application_details: Funding details are included in the official program offer and may combine scholarship, assistantship and tuition support.
min_academic_requirement: Admission to an eligible full-time doctoral program
verification_notes: Western's published minimum includes a living-support component and international tuition support; individual programs may offer more.

eligibility_summary: New international undergraduate students entering directly from secondary school with a very high admission average.
citizenship_requirement: Open to international students, including Pakistani citizens
application_details: No separate scholarship application; assessed from the admission record.
verification_notes: Published structure: CAD 15,000 in year 1 and CAD 7,500 in each of years 2–4, subject to renewal.

eligibility_summary: New international undergraduate students entering directly from secondary school in the published admission-average range.
citizenship_requirement: Open to international students, including Pakistani citizens
application_details: No separate scholarship application; assessed from the admission record.
verification_notes: Published structure: CAD 10,000 in year 1 and CAD 5,000 in each of years 2–4, subject to renewal.

eligibility_summary: Exceptional international secondary-school students with outstanding academics, volunteer service, extracurricular achievement and leadership.
citizenship_requirement: Open to international students, including Pakistani citizens
application_details: Apply to York, submit the international scholarship application and obtain the required school nomination.
verification_notes: Published value is CAD 45,000 per year for four years. Check the next-cycle deadlines.

eligibility_summary: New international students entering their first undergraduate degree directly from secondary school, with no previous post-secondary study. Award tier is determined by the final admission average.
citizenship_requirement: Open to international students, including Pakistani citizens
application_details: No separate scholarship application; eligible applicants are assessed from the admission file.
min_academic_requirement: 80% minimum; higher award bands begin at 85%, 90% and 95%
verification_notes: Official 2026 schedule: 80–84.9% = CAD 7,000 total; 85–89.9% = CAD 12,500; 90–94.9% = CAD 24,000; 95%+ = CAD 32,000. Renewal conditions apply.

eligibility_summary: Incoming international undergraduate students with strong leadership, community involvement and academic achievement.
citizenship_requirement: Open to international students, including Pakistani citizens
application_details: Submit the award application and supporting information through Brock's awards process.
deadline: Check current cycle on official page
verification_notes: Verified against the official university or official scholarship-program page on 20-Jul-2026.

eligibility_summary: International fee-paying students graduating from a secondary school outside Canada under an international curriculum; limited to the first eligible accepted students who complete the required deposit.
citizenship_requirement: Open to international students, including Pakistani citizens
application_details: Admission and deposit requirements must be completed; limited-number award.
verification_notes: Verified against the official university or official scholarship-program page on 20-Jul-2026.

eligibility_summary: New and continuing students, including international students where the individual award permits, are considered for admission, excellence, leadership and need-based awards.
citizenship_requirement: Open to international students, including Pakistani citizens
application_details: Use HEC Montréal's official scholarship directory and filter each award by study cycle and international eligibility.
verification_notes: This is an official searchable award pool. Each named award has its own value, deadline and citizenship rules, so the source should be refreshed before each intake.

eligibility_summary: Students admitted to the HEC Montréal PhD program, including international students, receive a four-year funding package subject to satisfactory academic progress.
citizenship_requirement: Open to international students, including Pakistani citizens
application_details: Funding is arranged through HEC Montréal after admission; consult the doctoral offer and funding conditions.
verification_notes: Official 2026–27 PhD description states at least CAD 120,000 over four years, generally CAD 30,000 per year, plus a separate activity-related tuition exemption described by the school.

amount_cad: Partial or full international tuition differential; value varies
eligibility_summary: Selected international graduate students in eligible HEC Montréal programs, based on academic excellence and program funding rules.

eligibility_summary: International students may access awards that do not impose Canadian citizenship or permanent-resident restrictions. Eligibility depends on study cycle, program and donor terms.
citizenship_requirement: Open to international students, including Pakistani citizens

eligibility_summary: Outstanding international student-athletes recruited for eligible TRU varsity teams and admitted to an academic program.

eligibility_summary: New international undergraduate students entering directly from secondary school. Award tier is based on the admission average.
